sentence_reviews.text: Program synthesis searches a space of executable programs from specifications, examples, tests, types, natural-language intent, or learned priors.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA search/programming background and ML sequence-modeling/probabilistic-prior evidence; program-synthesis-specific source is not cited.
sentence_reviews.notes: The definition is accurate, but the packet sources support it indirectly rather than through a dedicated synthesis reference.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Program synthesis & code search is to connect Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations, Graph search, BFS/DFS & A* to downstream capabilities including Code models, copilots & repository intelligence.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Search and sequence-modeling background; code-model downstream role is synthesized.
sentence_reviews.notes: The dependencies are plausible, but the exact repository-intelligence framing is not directly cited.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Code models, copilots & repository intelligence
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Transformer/sequence-modeling background; code-copilot-specific evidence is not cited.
sentence_reviews.notes: Program synthesis relates to code assistants, but the packet lacks direct code-model or repository-intelligence sources.

sentence_reviews.text: combined with Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA symbolic/search background; program IR details are not directly cited.
sentence_reviews.notes: Program synthesis naturally manipulates program representations, though the source support is indirect.

sentence_reviews.text: Search and self-evaluation can multiply cost while preserving confident errors when states, tools, or verifiers are weak. Its direct dependencies include Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations, Graph search, BFS/DFS & A*.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA search complexity and problem-space search background.
sentence_reviews.notes: Search cost is supported; self-evaluation/confident-error language is not directly sourced.

sentence_reviews.text: Program synthesis & code search uses Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Symbolic representation/search background; program-representation specificity is synthesized.
sentence_reviews.notes: A program-synthesis system must represent candidate programs, but the exact AST/IR phrasing is not directly cited.

sentence_reviews.text: Program synthesis & code search uses Graph search, BFS/DFS & A* as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.notes: Search over candidate programs is supported, but BFS/DFS/A* are examples rather than necessary ingredients.

sentence_reviews.text: Program synthesis & code search uses Decoder-only causal transformers as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.notes: Modern code generation can use decoder-only transformers, but program synthesis as a field does not require them.

sentence_reviews.text: Program synthesis & code search uses Constraint propagation, logic & program execution as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA constraint/search and logical reasoning background.
sentence_reviews.notes: Constraint-guided synthesis is plausible, but not universal and not directly sourced as this dependency.

edge_reviews.notes: Program representation is a core practical dependency, though direct AST/IR evidence is indirect.
Search is core, but BFS/DFS/A* are not the only or necessarily direct mechanisms.
Relevant to neural code generation, but not a general dependency of program synthesis.
Constraint execution is a common synthesis ingredient, but the evidence is indirect.
